mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above 4.5% within a single month, it signals deteriorating labor market conditions that compress consumer spending expectations, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment threshold breach.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous contraction in industrial production and housing starts indicates a synchronized demand-supply shock that erodes corporate profit margins, predicting negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint decline of INDPRO and HOUST fails to predict negative 60-day forward returns in more than 50% of observations over a 3-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: In a high-rate environment where the federal funds rate exceeds 4.0%, rising consumer credit balances reflect resilient household liquidity that supports equity valuations, yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns turn negative when FEDFUNDS remains above 4.0% and RSAFS increases month-over-month for three consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: Sequential quarterly growth in real GDP directly expands corporate earnings bases, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days as earnings revisions flow through to prices.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDP growth fails to exhibit a positive Spearman correlation with 20-day forward SPX returns across two full business cycles.

mechanism_narrative: When nonfarm payrolls fall below the 150,000 threshold for two consecutive months, it confirms a labor market cooling cycle that reduces aggregate demand, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS falls below 150000 for two consecutive months
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns remain positive or neutral in > 55% of instances following two consecutive months of sub-150k payroll growth.

mechanism_narrative: A divergence where industrial production rises while housing starts fall indicates a structural shift toward commercial investment over residential construction, which historically supports equity risk premiums and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The INDPRO-HOUST divergence fails to predict positive 5-day forward returns in more than 50% of rolling 12-month windows.

mechanism_narrative: During periods where the federal funds rate is rising but the unemployment rate remains below 4.2%, the labor market absorbs tightening without recessionary damage, allowing equity multiples to expand and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns turn negative when FEDFUNDS increases and UNRATE stays below 4.2% for more than 6 consecutive months.
